HIGH risk is mandatory for any work touching Intelligence Core (SOP §10). Validator extension also touches backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py — a listed HIGH-risk control-plane script.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md line 6: change_type=MIXED. MIXED governs via BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md line 5: execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. Correct — prompt requires a 5-command baseline preflight (lines 36-43) and a 6-command closure verification (lines 374-381) around the implementation body. HIGH-risk MIXED sprint requires kernel token governance.

docs/audit-papers/ARCH-LEGACY-1_pathway_retirement_audit.md: CONFIRMED present. Audit status: Complete (2026-05-31). Contains full 16-item legacy pathway inventory (L1-L16) with classification, reachability assessment, guardrail coverage assessment, and validator gap list at lines 114-120. Recommended retirement order confirmed at lines 141-147. No launch blockers identified.

No duplicate authority sources detected. Carry-forward register and ARCH-LEGACY-1 audit are the primary sprint authority sources; each covers a distinct scope. Compiled card evidence authority is exclusively in health_system_card_evidence.py:PILOT_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS (line 48).

backend/core/analytics/domain_narrative_wave1.py:438 — cv_contributor function defined. Grep search of backend/ for 'cv_contributor\b' returns only the definition line (line 438). Zero call sites confirmed. Safe to delete. ARCH-LEGACY-1 classification: retirement_candidate (audit line 39, L2). CF-MEDREV2-003 status: Open.

backend/core/knowledge/health_system_card_evidence.py:48 — PILOT_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS = WAVE1_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS = frozenset of all 7 Wave 1 subsystem IDs (lines 26-36). In assemble_wave1_subsystem_evidence (wave1_subsystem_evidence.py lines 159-186), every spec.subsystem_id iterates from WAVE1_DOMAIN_SUBSYSTEM_DEFS (lines 87-91) which defines exactly those 7 IDs. All 7 are in PILOT_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS, so the compiled branch (lines 160-167) always executes and `continue` is always reached. The fallback branch (lines 169-185, calling _partition_subsystem_markers at lines 129-139) is UNREACHABLE. CLARIFICATION: The _Wave1SubsystemDef dataclass (lines 25-29) and instances _WAVE1_CV_LIPID etc. (lines 32-91) ARE STILL REQUIRED — they define the subsystem_id values iterated by the compiled-path lookup. Cursor must NOT delete the _Wave1SubsystemDef class or instances. Only the fallback else-branch (lines 169-185) and the _partition_subsystem_markers function (lines 129-139) are dead and removable. Sprint prompt language 'remove unreachable _Wave1SubsystemDef hard-coded partition' must be interpreted as removing the partition FUNCTION and fallback CODE PATH only, not the subsystem definition class or instances.

backend/core/analytics/domain_score_assembler.py:580 — confidence_sentence_cv_coherent(tier, _contrib) called in all CV card assembly (both lipid-visible and non-lipid-visible paths). When lipid_visible=True (_lipid_visible path, lines 550-555), contributor comes from cv_contributor_for_lipid_visible_card which excludes hcy copy (_cv_contributor_lipid_only_signal_fallback, domain_narrative_wave1.py:494-508), so homocysteine bridge in confidence_sentence_cv_coherent (line 340: 'if "homocysteine" in c') will not fire. When lipid_visible=False (line 556-558), contributor comes from cv_contributor_primary which may include homocysteine via _cv_contributor_signal_fallback — bridge conditionally reachable. ARCH-LEGACY-1 classification: retained_reachable_classified (L5). Prompt Target 1 says to 'tighten invariant' not delete. This is correct.

backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py confirmed present. Existing pattern at lines 76-100 uses Python imports from core modules to assert compiled authority. validate_day_one_architecture.py is a HIGH-risk control-plane script per SOP §12. ARCH-LEGACY-1 validator gaps listed at audit lines 115-120: dead symbol detection, CRP migration status, root-cause promotion inventory, signal policy consistency, proof hard-coded subsystem partition unreachable. Prompt Target 3 scope (lines 203-213) is bounded: at minimum 5 guards to assess/implement. Prompt correctly adds 'do not add brittle source-text checks unless following existing validator pattern' — this is the right constraint.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 215-225: CRP migration default is 'Do not migrate CRP Pass 3 package path in this sprint unless hardening approves it as in scope.' Hardening assessment: CRP migration is NOT approved in scope. CF-ARCHLEG1-002 remains a migration_required item needing a dedicated sprint (CRP-PASS3-MIGRATION). The CRP path inspection and classification within this sprint is bounded to 'inspect and classify, but should not implement' per prompt line 219. Carry-forward register must retain CF-ARCHLEG1-002 as Open.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 133-141 (Scope), lines 146-225 (Specific implementation targets), lines 229-248 (Out of scope): scope is tightly bounded to 4 targets. Out-of-scope list explicitly excludes 15 categories including signal activation, scoring rails, clinical thresholds, SSOT, SignalEvaluator, LLM translation, UX redesign. No scope creep detected.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 253-265 (Required tests) — 9 regression targets defined. Lines 267-280 — explicit test commands: python backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py, python -m pytest backend/tests/architecture/test_day_one_architecture_guardrails.py -q plus targeted regression tests. All 3 existing regression test files confirmed present in repo.

Removing cv_contributor (dead function) cannot affect determinism — zero call sites. Removing _partition_subsystem_markers fallback path cannot affect runtime output — path is unreachable (all subsystems route compiled-first). confidence_sentence_cv_coherent hcy bridge remains — no behavioural change to existing output. Validator extensions are read-only assertions — cannot affect analytical output. Main determinism risk: if Cursor incorrectly deletes _Wave1SubsystemDef instances (see clarification in target2 check), the compiled-path loop would break. Clarification instruction above mitigates this.

health_system_card_evidence.py:45-47 — WAVE1_MED_REV1_HIDDEN_SUBSYSTEM_IDS = WAVE1_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS minus WAVE1_MED_REV1_SCORED_VISIBLE_SUBSYSTEM_IDS. This set is authoritative and not modified by this sprint. backend/tests/regression/test_med_rev1_wave1_subsystem_visibility.py is listed in required tests. Prompt STOP condition line 319 (item 7) explicitly blocks reintroduction of hidden subsystems.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 312-324: 9 STOP conditions all measurable. Particularly: STOP if ARCH-LEGACY-1 report missing (1), carry-forward register missing (2), removing legacy code affects live untested paths (3), fallback cannot be removed without broad redesign (4), ARCH-RT-6 validator fails (8). All verifiable without ambiguity.

Prompt closure requirements (lines 374-381) include explicit 'git stash list' check. Prompt lines 386-393 prohibit finish unless no ambiguous stash exists. Sprint scope does not reference .codex/, .vscode/, or AGENTS.md files. No tooling leakage risk identified.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 398-410: 9 success criteria all measurable — targeted legacy paths retired/retained with rationale (1), Wave 1 compiled path cannot silently fallback to hard-coded defs (2), CV legacy narrative cannot re-enter visible card score-basis (3), validator coverage improved (4), carry-forward register updated (5), no clinical scoring changes (6), ARCH-RT-6 validator passes (7), tests prove retirement protections (8), gate passes (9).
